Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine and proteinuria, suggesting possible renal impairment. Urine is cloudy, brown, with low specific gravity, and contains glucose, ketones, bilirubin, and protein, indicating possible tubular dysfunction or glomerular disease. Blood glucose is normal, but HbA1c is low, which may reflect recent changes or anemia. Electrolytes are mostly normal except for slightly high chloride. Recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for underlying renal pathology.
